Context.purpose: Ship v1 as a thin MCP proxy over tg-chat-rag with FastMCP runtime, OAuth-only authorization for /mcp, and an internal admin surface for operator login and diagnostics.
Context.scope: Expose search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, and list_sources on /mcp via FastMCP; provide /admin/login and /admin/ops via FastMCP's underlying Hono app; keep API key lifecycle out of product surface.

constraints.constraint-1: External MCP callers cannot provide filters.chat_ids.
constraints.constraint-2: search_messages always uses chat IDs from TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS env.
constraints.constraint-3: All upstream tg-chat-rag calls use Bearer token from TG_CHAT_RAG_BEARER_TOKEN.
constraints.constraint-4: /mcp authorization is enforced through FastMCP authenticate(request) backed by mcp-auth token verification.
constraints.constraint-5: ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN is valid only for /admin/login and must never authorize /mcp.
constraints.constraint-6: FastMCP HTTP Stream transport is the single MCP transport surface; endpoint is /mcp.
constraints.constraint-7: OAuth metadata must be published at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp using FastMCP oauth config.
constraints.constraint-8: OAuth client registration and refresh token lifecycle are owned by Logto; this server acts only as OAuth protected resource.
constraints.constraint-9: FastMCP v3.33.0 runtime route extension uses getApp() (Hono). Do not rely on addRoute runtime API.
constraints.constraint-10: API key CRUD and API key repository are removed from runtime architecture.
constraints.constraint-11: Tool parameter schemas remain deterministic and Standard Schema compatible (Zod-based in implementation).
constraints.constraint-12: No internal trip planning/orchestration/memory subsystem is required for v1 release.

contract.purpose: Load and validate runtime configuration for FastMCP runtime, admin authentication, OAuth resource-server validation, and upstream proxy calls.

export-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Validate env and return typed runtime config

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed app config with tg-chat-rag, admin auth, FastMCP runtime, and OAuth resource-server settings

notes.note-9: Required env: OAUTH_REQUIRED_SCOPES (CSV, default contains mcp:access)

contract.purpose: Provide structured logs for MCP requests, OAuth validation decisions, admin actions, upstream calls, and failures.
Create and expose Drizzle PostgreSQL database client from DATABASE_URL runtime configuration for current and upcoming data modules.

export-createDb.PURPOSE: Initialize Drizzle client and pooled PostgreSQL connection

type-DbClient.PURPOSE: Typed Drizzle database handle used by repositories

contract.purpose: Define and export MCP tool schemas and tool allowlist for the proxy surface using Standard Schema-compatible contracts.

export-SearchMessagesInputPublic.PURPOSE: Public schema for search_messages with filters.chat_ids forbidden

export-PROXIED_TOOL_NAMES.PURPOSE: Constant allowlist for proxied tool names

contract.purpose: Call tg-chat-rag methods API via POST /api/v1/methods/<tool_name> with service-level Bearer auth.

export-callMethod.PURPOSE: Execute proxied tool call to tg-chat-rag methods API

contract.purpose: Initialize mcp-auth with Logto OIDC discovery and expose framework-agnostic token verification and metadata utilities.

export-initMcpAuth.PURPOSE: Async init: fetch Logto OIDC metadata, create MCPAuth instance, expose verify/issuer utilities and metadata payloads

export-McpAuthContext.PURPOSE: Type for initialized auth context returned by initMcpAuth

notes.note-1: Uses fetchServerConfig(OAUTH_ISSUER, { type: 'oidc' }) to obtain Logto metadata at startup.
notes.note-2: Creates MCPAuth in resource-server mode and keeps metadata payload for FastMCP oauth config.
notes.note-3: JWKS caching/rotation remains delegated to jose through mcp-auth internals.

contract.purpose: Adapt mcp-auth verification to FastMCP authenticate(request) contract and return typed auth session payload for tool execution context.

export-authenticateFastMcpRequest.PURPOSE: FastMCP authenticate hook implementation: parse Bearer, verify token, validate issuer/audience/scopes, return session or throw HTTP error response

export-McpAuthSession.PURPOSE: Typed auth session context passed to FastMCP tool handlers

notes.note-1: Maps denied auth outcomes to deterministic 401/403 responses with WWW-Authenticate metadata challenges.
notes.note-2: No direct dependency on @modelcontextprotocol/sdk request auth types in runtime surface.

contract.purpose: Authenticate admin operators with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and enforce cookie session checks for /admin routes.

export-authenticateAdmin.PURPOSE: Verify login token and produce admin session cookie

export-requireAdminSession.PURPOSE: Check admin session and decide allow/redirect

export-clearAdminSession.PURPOSE: Clear admin auth cookie on logout

contract.purpose: Apply policy-safe normalization and proxy supported MCP tool calls to tg-chat-rag.

export-executeTool.PURPOSE: Validate args, inject internal chat_ids policy, call upstream, normalize result

contract.purpose: Render admin HTML with Bun JSX for operator login and OAuth runtime diagnostics.

export-renderAdminLayout.PURPOSE: Render operator layout with OAuth status tab

export-renderOpsStatus.PURPOSE: Render OAuth and MCP runtime status diagnostics

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.NAME: FastMcpRuntime

contract.purpose: Create and configure FastMCP runtime: register tools, bind authentication adapter, publish OAuth metadata, and mount admin routes via Hono app.

export-createFastMcpRuntime.PURPOSE: Build FastMCP instance with oauth/authenticate configuration and health endpoint

export-registerProxyTools.PURPOSE: Register proxied MCP tools on FastMCP with schemas from contracts

export-mountAdminRoutes.PURPOSE: Mount /admin routes on FastMCP's Hono app via getApp()

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER, M-ADMIN-UI

notes.note-1: Custom HTTP routes are mounted through FastMCP.getApp() because addRoute is not available in installed runtime API.
notes.note-2: FastMCP oauth config publishes well-known endpoints for authorization server and protected resource metadata.

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain

contract.purpose: Boot process runtime, initialize auth/provider dependencies, construct FastMCP runtime, and start HTTP stream server with graceful shutdown.

export-main.PURPOSE: Boot sequence with async mcp-auth init, FastMCP runtime wiring, and lifecycle management

M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-DB, M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER, M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-AUTH, M-ADMIN-UI, M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME

notes.note-1: main() calls initMcpAuth() at startup to fail fast on invalid OAuth configuration.
notes.note-2: Server.start() uses FastMCP transportType=httpStream and endpoint=/mcp.

contract.purpose: Unit verification of FastMCP authenticate adapter behavior for missing/invalid/insufficient/valid tokens and challenge responses.

export-McpAuthAdapterTests.PURPOSE: Checks adapter mapping from mcp-auth outcomes to FastMCP authenticate contract

contract.purpose: Integration verification of FastMCP well-known discovery routes, auth challenge flow, admin routes, and authorized MCP dispatch.

export-ServerIntegrationTests.PURPOSE: Validates discovery metadata, auth decisions, and MCP tool dispatch via FastMCP runtime

contract.purpose: Smoke verification for FastMCP tool registration surface and denied/allowed dispatch behavior.

export-FastMcpRuntimeSmokeTests.PURPOSE: Checks tool list exposure, schema validation, and dispatch routing through proxy service

DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.NAME: Admin Login Flow
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-1: Operator submits login form with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN candidate.
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH validates token and issues signed HTTP-only admin session cookie.
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-3: Admin route mounted on FastMCP Hono app redirects authenticated operator to /admin/ops.

DF-ADMIN-GUARD.NAME: Admin Route Guard Flow
DF-ADMIN-GUARD.step-1: Request arrives for admin route on FastMCP Hono app.
DF-ADMIN-GUARD.step-4: Authenticated request is routed to M-ADMIN-UI handlers.

DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.NAME: OAuth Protected Resource Discovery Flow
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-1: MCP client requests protected resource metadata for /mcp.
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME serves metadata from FastMCP oauth configuration.
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-3: Client starts OAuth authorization against Logto and obtains access token.

DF-MCP-AUTH.NAME: MCP OAuth Auth Flow
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-1: Client sends Authorization header with OAuth Bearer access token.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-2: FastMCP invokes authenticate(request) for the HTTP stream request.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-3: M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER extracts Bearer token and calls mcp-auth verify + issuer/audience/scopes validation.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-4: Unauthorized request returns 401/403 with WWW-Authenticate challenge metadata.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-5: Authorized request receives auth session context and proceeds to tool dispatch.

DF-PROXY-SEARCH.NAME: search_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool search_messages.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME validates parameters against public schema that forbids filters.chat_ids.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-4: M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT sends POST {TG_CHAT_RAG_BASE_URL}/api/v1/methods/search_messages with Bearer token.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-5: Response is normalized and returned to MCP client.

DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.NAME: get_message_context Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_message_context.
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME validates payload with M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS schema.
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-RELATED.NAME: get_related_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_related_messages.
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME validates payload with M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS schema.
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-SOURCES.NAME: list_sources Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool list_sources.
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME validates payload with M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS schema.
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.NAME: list_chats Exclusion Flow
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-2: M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME does not register list_chats in tool registry.
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-3: Client receives MCP tool-not-found behavior; no upstream call is performed.

Phase-1.name: Existing Proxy Foundation
Phase-1.step-1: Implemented typed env parsing and strict validation for TG_CHAT_RAG_* variables.
Phase-1.step-2: Implemented structured logger and request-scoped fields.
Phase-1.step-3: Implemented public schemas and proxied tool allowlist constants.
Phase-1.step-4: Implemented upstream tg-chat-rag adapter with timeout and normalized errors.

Phase-2.name: OAuth Contract Rewrite
Phase-2.step-1: Extended config contract with PUBLIC_URL and OAuth validation settings.
Phase-2.step-2: Replaced API key management scope with operator diagnostics scope.

Phase-3.name: OAuth Core Validation
Phase-3.step-1: Implemented provider-level OIDC discovery and token verification context.
Phase-3.step-2: Wired auth verification and OAuth challenge behavior for /mcp.

Phase-4.name: Legacy API Key Removal
Phase-4.step-1: Removed API key repository dependency from /mcp auth path and startup wiring.
Phase-4.step-2: Removed /admin/api-keys routes, forms, and fragments from operator UI.

Phase-5.name: mcp-auth Library Migration
Phase-5.step-1: Removed obsolete JWKS cache/clock-skew env controls no longer used after mcp-auth cutover.
Phase-5.step-2: Adopted mcp-auth driven verification context and metadata payloads.
Phase-5.step-3: Updated startup/auth route wiring to provider-backed flow.

Phase-6.name: FastMCP Architectural Planning
Phase-6.step-1: Defined FastMCP runtime as replacement for manual MCP transport layer.
Phase-6.step-2: Defined FastMCP authenticate adapter contract over mcp-auth verification.
Phase-6.step-3: Defined server bootstrap contract around FastMCP start/stop lifecycle.

Phase-7.name: FastMCP Migration Execution
Phase-7.step-1: Create src/auth/mcp-auth-adapter.ts and implement authenticateFastMcpRequest() over McpAuthContext with deterministic 401/403 mapping.
Phase-7.step-2: Create src/runtime/fastmcp-runtime.ts: instantiate FastMCP, register four tools with schemas, bind authenticate hook, configure oauth metadata, health endpoint, and admin routes via getApp().
Phase-7.step-3: Refactor src/server/index.ts to bootstrap dependencies and call fastmcp.start({ transportType: 'httpStream', httpStream: { endpoint: '/mcp' } }).
Phase-7.step-4: Remove legacy manual MCP transport/guard wiring from runtime request path after parity tests pass.
Phase-7.step-5: Add smoke tests for tool registration, schema rejects, and authorized dispatch through proxy service.
Phase-7.step-6: Add adapter unit tests for missing header, malformed bearer, invalid token, insufficient scope, and valid token.
Phase-7.step-7: Refresh integration tests to validate FastMCP well-known routes, /admin, and /mcp end-to-end auth+dispatch flow.
Phase-7.step-8: Delete src/transport/mcp-transport.ts and src/auth/mcp-auth-guard.ts after successful cutover and test stability.

RISK-1.name: FastMCP Route API Mismatch
RISK-1.impact: Installed fastmcp v3.33.0 runtime exposes getApp() but not addRoute(), while docs/examples may suggest addRoute().
RISK-1.mitigation: Use only getApp() (Hono) for custom /admin routes. Add runtime assertion test that route mounting works via getApp().

RISK-2.name: Auth Challenge Parity Drift
RISK-2.impact: Moving from manual /mcp guard to FastMCP authenticate hook can change 401/403 response shape and WWW-Authenticate fields.
RISK-2.mitigation: Codify challenge behavior in adapter tests and server integration tests. Keep reason-code mapping deterministic.

RISK-3.name: IncomingMessage Header Normalization Errors
RISK-3.impact: FastMCP authenticate receives IncomingMessage where headers may be string|string[]; parsing mistakes can cause false auth denies.
RISK-3.mitigation: Centralize Authorization parsing helper in adapter and cover array/undefined cases in unit tests.

RISK-4.name: Endpoint Ownership Conflicts
RISK-4.impact: FastMCP internal handling for /mcp and /.well-known/* can conflict with manually mounted routes if duplicated.
RISK-4.mitigation: Reserve MCP and well-known routes for FastMCP config only; mount custom app routes strictly under /admin and optional health endpoint.

RISK-5.name: Tool Access Session Shape Drift
RISK-5.impact: Tool canAccess/session behavior can break if auth session object shape changes during adapter implementation.
RISK-5.mitigation: Define and export stable McpAuthSession type in adapter and consume that type in runtime/tool contracts.

RISK-6.name: Startup Dependency on OIDC Discovery
RISK-6.impact: initMcpAuth() performs remote OIDC discovery at startup; transient network issues can block boot.
RISK-6.mitigation: Fail fast with explicit diagnostics; consider optional deferred discovery strategy if startup availability becomes critical.
